scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT COURT / CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris, aged about 45 years, and is competent to institute this suit. The Defendant is a person sui juris, aged about 38 years, and can be sued in his individual capacity. The Defendant resides at Koramangala, Bengaluru, and the cause of action arose within Bengaluru where the loan was advanced and the legal notice was issued. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as the Defendant resides and the cause of action arose within its limits. The suit value is within the pecuniary jurisdiction of this Court. This Hon'ble Court has subject matter jurisdiction under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 to try all suits of a civil nature.

LIMITATION

2. The present suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, as the loan was repayable on a specific date (20.06.2024) and the suit is instituted within three years from said date.

FACTS OF THE TRANSACTION

3. On 15.03.2024, the Defendant requested financial assistance from the Plaintiff. Acting on this request, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. 15,00,000 to the Defendant as a hand loan via NEFT bank transfer bearing UTR Number AXIB20240315123456, as evidenced by Annexure A. There was no written agreement executed between the parties; however, the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the loan amount via WhatsApp messages, as evidenced by Annexure B. It was orally agreed between the parties that the loan amount was repayable on demand or latest by 20.06.2024.

DEFAULT AND DEMAND

4. The repayment of the loan amount became due on 20.06.2024 as per the oral terms agreed between the parties. The Defendant failed and refused to repay the outstanding amount despite the due date passing. The Plaintiff made repeated oral demands for repayment, and subsequently issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, as evidenced by Annexure C. The Defendant has failed to respond satisfactorily to the legal notice and has failed to repay the loan amount despite having the means to do so.

QUANTIFICATION OF CLAIM

5. The principal amount due and payable by the Defendant to the Plaintiff is Rs. 15,00,000. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the principal amount from the date of default i.e., 20.06.2024 till the date of realisation. The interest claimed is simple interest and not compound interest, as there was no express agreement for compound interest. The total claim amount comprises the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000 plus accrued interest calculated at 12% per annum from 20.06.2024, totaling {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}} as on the date of filing this plaint.

LEGAL BASIS

6. The Plaintiff is entitled to recovery under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, as the Defendant has committed a breach of the obligation to repay the loan. The Plaintiff is entitled to sue in this Court under Section 9 and Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as the cause of action arose within the jurisdiction and the Defendant resides herein. The claim for consideration is substantiated by the documentary trail of the NEFT transfer (Annexure A), pre-empting any defence of no consideration. The suit is within limitation as the Defendant acknowledged the debt via WhatsApp messages and the legal notice was issued within the statutory period. The claim for interest at 12% per annum is lawful and within market norms for unsecured lending, pre-empting any defence of usurious interest. The Plaintiff is entitled to pendente lite and future interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

7. The cause of action first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant failed to repay the loan amount as agreed, and subsequently on 01.07.2024 when the legal notice was issued and ignored by the Defendant. The cause of action consists of the Defendant's omission to repay the lawful debt despite demand and notice. The suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed by law, as the debt was acknowledged by the Defendant and the demand was made within the statutory period.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

8. For the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee, the suit is valued at {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}. This valuation covers the principal amount and accrued interest claimed in the plaint. The requisite court fee of {{CO
